rawText: On 10 July 2026 NutraIngredients reported that UK personalised-supplement brand Nourished (owned by Birmingham-based Rem3dy Health) launched three stacked creatine gummies at an RRP of £17.99 for 90 gummies, a 30-day supply, at 1 g of creatine per gummy, with a citicoline-containing cognition SKU; CEO Melissa Snover credited emerging cognition research with taking creatine 'from a specific cohort […] to really being very mass market'. The same article notes EFSA rejected the creatine-for-cognitive-function health claim in December 2024, concluding a cause-and-effect relationship could not be established, and reports that NOW's testing found half the creatine gummies it tested missed dosage claims while fitness influencer James Smith's testing found just 4 of 9 brands contained adequate creatine. Nourished says its products are third-party tested by Eurofins with creatine stability proven over 24 months of accelerated testing via patented encapsulation. · ANGLES: (1) Gummy dose arithmetic — 90 gummies over a 30-day supply is 3 gummies a day at 1 g each, the bottom of the commonly studied 3–5 g/day maintenance range. THIS DIVISION IS OURS, NOT THE SOURCE'S: the article does not state a daily dose; verify against the pack before publishing. (2) Cognition marketing against a standing EFSA rejection — what the regulator actually concluded in December 2024. (3) Third-party testing done properly — Nourished's Eurofins testing and stability data is a real answer to the gummy-dose problem and should be said so; this is a fair-play story, not a hit. · APP CONNECTION: check dose — paste the product and the app compares the per-serving dose against the studied dose for the claimed benefit. · SECONDARY: none independently fetched. Naming discrepancy in the source: the image caption lists 'Creatine Strength, Creatine Focus and Creatine Energy' while the body describes 'Creatine Mind' — reported as-is, resolve before naming SKUs. · SOURCE GRADE: B (trade journalism, date-verified 10-Jul-2026).
